title: Troubleshoot Snowflake OAuth Connectivity for Client Accounts
description:
Snowflake connectivity was failing when authenticating with the same account used to create the Security Integration (OAuth client ID and secret). The issue was reproduced: authenticating with the integration-creator account returned an error, while a different service account (SYSTEM_DBT) authenticated successfully.

### Root Cause
The account that created the Snowflake Security Integration had elevated permissions that conflicted with the OAuth authentication flow. Using a lower-privileged service account (e.g., SYSTEM_DATATOOLS) resolved the issue.

### Resolution
Create a dedicated `SYSTEM_DATATOOLS` user with appropriately scoped permissions for the OAuth flow, rather than using the admin account that created the integration.
acceptance_criteria: Snowflake OAuth authentication succeeds with a dedicated service account
Authentication does not fail due to elevated permissions on the integration-creator account
Client accounts can connect to Snowflake through the app
implementation_notes: The Snowflake Security Integration creator account has elevated permissions that interfere with OAuth token exchange. A lower-privilege service account works correctly. The fix was to create a dedicated SYSTEM_DATATOOLS user with appropriate read-level permissions.
comment_summary: Developer reproduced the issue and identified that the account used to create the Security Integration could not authenticate via OAuth, while a different account (SYSTEM_DBT) worked. The root cause was elevated permissions. Resolution was to create a dedicated lower-privilege service account.
